data.title: Advent International preparing bid for UK's Tate & Lyle - FT
data.description:
Private equity firm Advent International is preparing a bid for the UK's Tate & Lyle (OTCQX:TATYF) that is expected to value the ingredients maker at more than its £2.8bn market cap.

The PE shop is at the early stages of preparing an offer, according to a Financial Times report on Wednesday, which cited people familiar with the matter. Advent has yet to make the bid.

A potential bid come as Tate & Lyle (OTCQX:TATYF) is in the process of a $1.8 billion acquisition of pectin and specialty gums provider CP Kelco.

Shares of Tate & Lyle (OTCQX:TATYF) rose more than 12% on the report, though they were halted.

Representatives for Advent and Tate & Lyle declined to comment to the FT.

data.title: Saudi group seeking deal to buy stake in First Quantum's Zambian mines - Reuters
data.description:
Saudi Arabia's Manara Minerals is in advanced talks to buy a minority stake in First Quantum Minerals' (OTCPK:FQVLF) Zambian copper and nickel assets, Reuters reported Wednesday.

Manara, a joint venture between Saudi mining company Ma'aden and the Saudi Public Investment Fund, could conclude a deal by year-end to acquire a 15%-20% equity stake in the assets, which could be worth $1.5B-$2B, according to the report.

First Quantum (OTCPK:FQVLF) owns the Kansanshi and Sentinel copper mines in Zambia, which have become key to future production after last year's shutdown of its flagship Cobre Panama mine; the company also owns the Enterprise nickel mine in Zambia.

The Zambian mines contributed $1.08B to First Quantum's (OTCPK:FQVLF) revenue in Q2, and the company has said it plans to spend an additional $1.3B at the Kansanshi site over the next five years.

Manara has made significant investments in industrial metals and is in talks with Pakistan's government for a stake in the Reko Diq copper mine currently under development, which is owned by Barrick Gold and Pakistan state enterprises.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Wednesday: NVAX, UAL, ASML, JBHT, and more
data.description:
Stock futures were mixed on Wednesday, as Wall Street struggled to regain its footing after a dip in the previous session, largely weighed down by losses in chipmaker stocks.

Here are some of Wednesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • J.B. Hunt Transport Services (JBHT) shares gained 6% following the release of better-than-expected Q3 earnings. Despite a 2.8% Y/Y decline in revenue, driven by factors such as lower revenue per load and load volume, J.B. Hunt's Q3 performance was bolstered by growth in certain segments, such as JBI load growth and increased revenue per load in ICS. Excluding fuel surcharge revenue, total operating revenue decreased by less than 1% compared to the previous year.
  • U.S. Bancorp (USB) shares climbed 5% after the bank's Q3 earnings exceeded market expectations, fueled by sequential growth in net interest income. The bank expects Q4 net interest income to remain "relatively stable" at $4.17B, compared to the consensus of $4.10B. For the full year, it reaffirmed its 2024 net interest income guidance at $16.1B-$16.4B vs. the Visible Alpha's $16.2B consensus. Additionally, the bank maintained its mid-single-digit growth outlook for adjusted non-interest income while revising its adjusted non-interest expense guidance to $16.8B, consistent with its prior projection.
  • United Airlines' (UAL) shares surged as much as 8% after the company topped consensus in Q3 results. The airline also announced plans to repurchase $1.5B worth of shares, marking its first buyback since before the COVID-19 pandemic. For Q4, United projected earnings between $2.50 and $3.00 per share, with a midpoint of $2.75—up from $2.00 a year ago.

Biggest stock losers

  • ASML (ASML) shares fell over 5% after the company reported weaker-than-expected Q3 bookings and issued a cautious forecast for 2025, citing tighter export controls in the Netherlands. Bookings for the quarter plunged 53% sequentially to €2.63B, significantly missing the consensus of €5.39B. Despite this, ASML posted strong results in other areas, with net sales rising 20% to €7.47B, surpassing estimates of €7.17B, and net income soaring 32% to €2.08B, beating expectations of €1.91B. While Q4 sales projections remain optimistic, with an estimated midpoint of €9B slightly above the €8.95B estimate, the company lowered the net sales forecast for FY2025 to €30B to €35B (midpoint of €32.5B) from the prior view €30B to €40B.
  • Interactive Brokers Group (IBKR) shares slipped 4% after the company reported mixed Q3 results, falling short on earnings while exceeding revenue expectations with 20% Y/Y growth. Net interest income came in at $802M, up from $792M in Q2 and $733M in the same quarter last year. However, the company's net interest margin declined to 2.37%, compared to 2.42% in Q2 and 2.46% in Q3 of the previous year.

  • Shares of Novavax (NVAX) plummeted nearly 23% after the U.S. FDA placed a clinical hold on the new drug application for its COVID-19 and influenza combination vaccine and standalone influenza vaccine candidates, following a report of motor neuropathy in a Phase 2 trial participant outside the U.S. The participant received the vaccine in January 2023 and developed the condition in September 2024, after the trial concluded in July. Novavax stated that no previous trials have indicated motor neuropathy and that the hold does not affect the investigational new drug application for its standalone COVID-19 vaccine, while the company works with the FDA to resolve the issue.

data.title: Sadot Group to execute 1-for-10 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Sadot Group (NASDAQ:SDOT) will implement a 1-for-10 reverse stock split of its common stock on October 18, 2024.
  • Every 10 shares of Sadot's (SDOT) issued and outstanding common stock will be automatically converted into one issued and outstanding share of common stock without any change in the par value of $0.0001 per share.
  • The reverse stock split is primarily intended to bring the company into compliance with the minimum $1.00 per share requirement for continued listing on Nasdaq.
  • The split will reduce the number of issued and outstanding shares of the company's common stock from ~57.4M shares to ~5.74M shares.

data.title: Citizens Financial Q3 earnings, revenue slide as interest income falls sequentially
data.description:
Citizens Financial Group (NYSE:CFG) shares dipped 2.3% in Wednesday premarket trading after the lender turned in Q3 earnings and revenue that retreated both Q/Q and Y/Y as net interest income fell during the quarter, mainly due to the impact of forward starting swaps.

"While Q3 was impacted by the drag from forward-starting swaps that commenced in July, as well as some fees that pushed out to Q4, we project a strong fourth quarter and launch into 2025,” said Chairman and CEO Bruce Van Saun.

For Q4 2024, the bank expects net interest income to rise 1.5%-2.5% from Q3's $1.37B, implying $1.39B-$1.40B, according to a slide presentation. The guidance assumes the Federal Reserve will cut rates by 25 basis-points in November and December.

Noninterest income for the three-month period is expected to rise in the mid-to-high single digits from $534M in Q3, the slides showed. Noninterest expense is seen growing about 2% from $1.25B in the prior quarter.

Meanwhile, Q3 underlying EPS of $0.79, matching the consensus, fell from $0.82 in Q2 and $0.89 in Q3 2023.

Q3 total revenue of $1.90B, falling short of the $1.94B expected, fell from $1.96B in Q2 and $2.01B in Q3 of last year.

Net interest income fell 3% sequentially and 10% from a year ago to $1.37B. Net interest margin (fully taxable equivalent) of 2.77% vs. 2.87% in Q2 and 3.03% in Q3 2023..

Noninterest income fell to $532M from $553M in the previous quarter and climbed from $492M in the year-earlier period. Noninterest expense of $1.26B drifted down from $1.30B in Q2 and $1.29B in Q3 2023.

Provision for credit losses was $172M, compared with $182M in Q2 and $172M a year before.

Period-end loans and leases were $141.6B vs. $141.8B in Q2. Period-end deposits of $175.2B vs. $176.4B in Q2.

data.title: Brexit cost London's financial center almost 40K jobs, City chief says - report
data.description:
The UK's rocky exit from the European Union cost London's financial district almost 40,000 jobs, with Dublin and cities in continental Europe such as Paris and Milan benefitting, according to a media report published on Wednesday.

Michael Mainelli, who holds the title Lord Mayor of the City of London and has spent years keeping track of the UK financial district's fortunes, told Reuters, "Brexit was a disaster. We had 525,000 workers in 2016. My estimate is that we lost just short of 40,000."

The City of London stretches over a square mile in London and is home to the Bank of England, international banks, and insurers. The Lord Mayor is a largely ceremonial title.

Mainelli's estimate of job losses far surpasses the 7,000 jobs that the financial center lost by 2022, as calculated by EY consultants. UK citizens voted in 2016 to leave the EU. It took until 2019 for Brexit to take effect.

Still, the City of London is growing, including sectors other than finance. Worker ranks have increased to 615K as insurers and data analysis businesses grow, he told Reuters.

Dublin gained the most jobs, as a result of Brexit, gaining 10K positions, Mainelli said. Other cities that benefited include Milan, Paris, and Amsterdam.

Relevant tickers include British banks Barclays (NYSE:BCS), Lloyds Banking Group (NYSE:LYG), and HSBC (NYSE:HSBC). Related ETFs: [FXB] [EWU].

data.title: Aspen Aerogels sees Q3 revenue of $117M
data.description:
• Aspen Aerogels (NYSE:ASPN) on Wednesday said it expects Q3 revenue of around $117M.
  • Consensus for Q3 revenue is $95.07M.
  • ASPN +27.31% premarket to $28.57.
  • The company also said it expects quarterly net loss of ~$13M and quarterly adjusted EBITDA of about $25M.
  • Cash and cash equivalents were ~$113M as of September 30, 2024.
  • The company announced that it has received a conditional commitment from the U.S. Department of Energy for a proposed loan of up to $670.6M for financing the construction of its second aerogel manufacturing facility in Georgia.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow are muted after Wall Street closes lower on tech sell off
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages trade cautiously, a day after chip stocks slumped on a disappointing outlook from ASML (ASML).

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was near even, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.1%, and the Dow (DJI) was unchanged.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.01%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 2 basis points to 3.92%.

Wall Street closed in the red on Tuesday, as chip stocks took it on the chin following disappointing earnings from Dutch giant ASML (ASML) and news that the U.S. was mulling further export caps.

"Some rare weakness has come through in risk over the last 24 hours, with the S&P 500 falling back from its record high the previous day," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

ASML (ASML) shares were down 4% in premarket trade, after the firm said it expects 2025 net sales forecast to be between €30B to €35B (mid-point of €32.5B). The Dutch company also missed its third-quarter bookings estimates.

Morgan Stanley (MS) delivered a beat on both its top and bottom line in its third quarter, as banks continued to shine.

U.S. import and export prices fell more than expected in September.

data.title: Maxeon CEO Bill Mulligan to retire early next year
data.description:
• Maxeon Solar Technologies (NASDAQ:MAXN) on Wednesday announced a leadership transition, with CEO Bill Mulligan set to retire at the end of January 2025.
  • George Guo, formerly an advisor to the chairman of TCL Group and previous CEO of TCL Communication Technology, assumed the role of Maxeon CEO and joined its board of directors effective October 11, 2024.
  • Mulligan will aid the leadership transition through January.

data.title: S&W Seed Company provides update on delay of Form 10-K filing for FY 2024
data.description:
• S&W Seed Company (NASDAQ:SANW) on Wednesday, provided an update regarding the late filing of its annual report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended June 30, 2024.
  • The company said it is continuing to work through reporting requirements as a component of the previously announced voluntary plan of administration for its subsidiary, S&W Seed Company Australia Pty Ltd.
  • Due to the change in its reportable segments, the company is analyzing the impacts of change under ASC 250, Accounting Changes and Error Corrections, and is working to prepare all necessary segment disclosures with a goal to filing its 10-K as soon as possible.
